en.extract:
The chestnut-crowned bush warbler (Cettia major) is a species of warbler found in South Asia.
Although overall population size has not been quantified, this is one of the species that is declining due to habitat destruction. Although its population trend is observed to be decreasing, its decline is not happening in rapid fashion. For this reason, this is evaluated as least concern species.
The phylogenetic relationships among the Cettia species are shown below, based on the data by the Cornell Lab of Ornithology.

It split from the Chestnut-headed tesia about 5 million years ago.

The '''chestnut-crowned bush warbler''' ('''''Cettia major''''') is a species of warbler found in [[South Asia]].
Although overall population size has not been quantified, this is one of the species that is declining due to [[habitat destruction]].<ref>{{cite web|title=Chestnut-crowned Bush-warbler (Cettia major)|url=http://www.birdlife.org/datazone/speciesfactsheet.php?id=7550|access-date=1 Nov 2012}}</ref> Although its population trend is observed to be decreasing, its decline is not happening in rapid fashion. For this reason, this is evaluated as [[least concern]] species.<ref name="iucn status 12 November 2021" />

{{Phylogeny/Cettia}}It split from the [[Chestnut-headed tesia]] about 5 million years ago.

zh.extract:
大树莺（学名：Cettia major）为鶲科树莺属的鸟类。该物种分布在南亚地区，其模式产地在尼泊尔。


亚种
大树莺指名亚种（学名：Cettia major major）。在中国大陆，分布于西藏、四川、云南等地。该物种的模式产地在尼泊尔。
